purpose: Verified transcript-backed source packet for Telos hardware, formal-methods, stochastic-compute, and uncertainty-modeling research lanes.

source.title: The Thermodynamic AI Chip - Thomas Ahle

gather_receipt.harvest_method: yt-dlp sidecars, then gather parse offline

public_source_receipts.title: Building an Open-Source Verilog Simulator with AI
A First Demonstration of Thermodynamic Matrix Inversion
thermox: The First Thermodynamic Computing Simulator

telos_fit.summary: Strong Telos fit as an instrumentation and verification lane, not as evidence that Telos can design chips by itself.

gather: intake video, captions, related papers, repositories, and source pages while preserving method and content hashes
index: map local and public code, specs, papers, formal tools, and generated artifacts into a rerunnable workspace graph
forum: route subproblems across hardware verification, formal methods, probabilistic computing, program synthesis, and uncertainty modeling
crucible: score claims and outputs as MATCH, DRIFT, or UNVERIFIABLE against tests, proofs, simulations, or source receipts
telos: build shared human/model work surfaces for specs, simulations, stochastic traces, uncertainty meters, and replayable demos

integration_boundary.scope: Build public, source-backed adapters, demos, fixtures, and verification surfaces that can interoperate with Normal Computing-adjacent public research, formal methods, stochastic simulation, and hardware-verification workflows without implying affiliation or access to private materials.

integration_strategy.intent: Help advance the work by making public specs, simulations, tests, formal artifacts, uncertainty traces, and stochastic-compute demos more inspectable, replayable, and verifiable.

modules.description: Maintain a lawful public-source graph for Thomas Ahle and Normal Computing materials, including papers, blog posts, repositories, simulators, and transcript receipts.
Wrap AI-generated Verilog, CIRCT-style simulation outputs, formal checks, mutation tests, and mismatch reports as append-only action receipts.
Prototype seeded stochastic traces, noise-as-computation visualizations, matrix-inversion demos, and thermodynamic simulator artifacts with replayable seeds and uncertainty fields.
Expose Bayesian uncertainty, variance collapse, stochastic drift, confidence mismatch, and posterior-like summaries as explicit meters instead of hidden model intuition.
Let one behavior travel through tests, executable simulation, TLA+ sketch, timed Petri net sketch, Lean theorem stub, and human prose while recording formalization drift.

success_criteria: Every promoted technical claim has a public source receipt or a local reproducible check.
Every demo has deterministic seeds, artifact hashes, and MATCH/DRIFT/UNVERIFIABLE verdicts.
Tests and proofs are separated: passing tests never becomes proof of correct recovered structure.
Human-facing visualizations expose uncertainty and stochastic state without hiding source refs or raw private materials.

build_implications.description: Represent one behavior as tests, TLA+, Petri net sketch, Lean theorem stub, and executable simulation.
Show a generated function that passes many tests but fails a hidden invariant.
Record human spec, formal spec, proof or test result, and mismatch verdict.
Visualize seeded noise, stochastic differential dynamics, and uncertainty propagation.
Combine model proposals with fast deterministic search and check loops.

MATCH: gather captured metadata and transcript receipts and verified both stored bodies
transcript contains the core terms and themes in this packet
INFERRED: Telos can help by wrapping external formal, simulation, and verification tools with receipts and routing, not by replacing those tools.
UNVERIFIABLE_FROM_THIS_PACKET: whether the technical claims about Normal Computing, ProgramBench, or thermodynamic chips are correct outside the interview

next_actions: gather.run: resolve linked papers, repositories, company materials, and formal-method references.
index.context.envelope: map local formalization, simulation, and verification tools that can host the lab fixtures.
forum.route: split follow-up work across formal methods, hardware verification, stochastic compute, and uncertainty modeling lanes.
crucible.assess: add negative fixtures for tests-passing-with-wrong-structure, formalization drift, stochastic trace mismatch, and unverifiable hardware claims.
telos.context.pack: include this packet as a source ref for the next second-brain context handoff.
